questionStem: I understand what is expected of me when priorities or ways of working change.
insightPrompt: What is usually unclear when priorities or ways of working change?

questionStem: I have enough support to build confidence when new expectations, tools, or processes are introduced.
insightPrompt: What support is missing when new expectations, tools, or processes are introduced?

questionStem: In the past 30 days, how often did shifting priorities or change requests disrupt your focus or progress?
insightPrompt: What specifically caused the disruption when priorities shifted?

questionStem: Which statement best reflects how change is usually introduced in your work?
fc_optionA_label: Change is usually introduced clearly enough for me to adjust with reasonable support.
fc_optionA_insightPrompt: What support or clarity is helping you adapt well?
fc_optionB_label: When change happens, I often need more clarity, support, or time to adjust.
fc_optionB_insightPrompt: What support, communication, or clarity is missing when change happens?

questionStem: I feel safe speaking up about concerns, risks, or ideas that could improve the work.
insightPrompt: What makes it difficult to speak up about concerns, risks, or ideas?

questionStem: When I raise a concern, I trust that it will be handled respectfully.
insightPrompt: What makes it hard to trust that concerns will be handled respectfully?

questionStem: In the past 30 days, how often did you hold back from raising a concern, idea, or mistake?
insightPrompt: Why did you hold back from speaking up?

questionStem: Which statement best reflects what usually happens when concerns are raised at work?
fc_optionA_label: Concerns can usually be raised and addressed in a respectful way.
fc_optionA_insightPrompt: Can you give an example of when follow-through worked well in your area?
fc_optionB_label: Raising concerns can sometimes feel uncertain, difficult, or unlikely to lead to action.
fc_optionB_insightPrompt: What makes speaking up feel risky, unsafe, or ineffective?

questionStem: People I work with communicate openly enough to prevent confusion or tension.
insightPrompt: Where does communication tend to break down?

questionStem: I can raise concerns directly with coworkers when something is not working.
insightPrompt: What makes it difficult to raise concerns directly?

questionStem: In the past 30 days, how often did miscommunication or unresolved tension slow down your work?
insightPrompt: What caused the miscommunication or unresolved tension?

questionStem: Which statement best reflects how your team works together?
fc_optionA_label: People usually communicate clearly and work through issues when needed.
fc_optionA_insightPrompt: What helps your team communicate and resolve issues well?
fc_optionB_label: Communication gaps or unresolved issues sometimes make teamwork harder than it needs to be.
fc_optionB_insightPrompt: What makes difficult conversations or issue resolution hard on your team?

questionStem: I understand the top priorities that guide my work.
insightPrompt: What is unclear about the priorities that guide your work?

questionStem: I understand how my work connects to broader team and organizational goals.
insightPrompt: What makes it hard to see how your work connects to broader goals?

questionStem: In the past 30 days, how often did you continue work that later turned out to be a lower priority?
insightPrompt: What caused lower-priority work to continue?

questionStem: Which statement best reflects how priorities show up in your day-to-day work?
fc_optionA_label: Priorities are usually clear enough for me to focus on the right work.
fc_optionA_insightPrompt: What helps keep priorities clear and actionable for you?
fc_optionB_label: Competing requests or changing direction sometimes make priorities harder to judge.
fc_optionB_insightPrompt: What is driving competing priorities or confusion about what matters most?

questionStem: I usually know who is responsible for each part of the work I depend on.
insightPrompt: Where is ownership or accountability unclear?

questionStem: The processes I use most often are clear enough to follow without frequent workarounds.
insightPrompt: Which processes are unclear or hard to follow?

questionStem: In the past 30 days, how often did work stall because ownership, process steps, or approvals were unclear?
insightPrompt: What typically causes work to stall?

questionStem: Which statement best reflects how work flows across your area?
fc_optionA_label: Work usually moves forward with enough clarity around roles and next steps.
fc_optionA_insightPrompt: What helps work flow smoothly in your area?
fc_optionB_label: Work sometimes slows because roles, ownership, or next steps are not fully clear.
fc_optionB_insightPrompt: Where do role clarity or process clarity break down most often?

questionStem: I usually have enough time, staffing, and support to do my work well.
insightPrompt: What resources or supports are missing most often?

questionStem: Workloads are distributed fairly enough that work can be completed sustainably.
insightPrompt: Where does workload distribution feel unfair or unsustainable?

questionStem: In the past 30 days, how often did lack of time, staffing, or support prevent you from doing quality work?
insightPrompt: What resource gaps most affected your ability to do quality work?

questionStem: Which statement best reflects how workload and resources are experienced in your work?
fc_optionA_label: Resources and workload are usually managed well enough to support steady delivery.
fc_optionA_insightPrompt: What helps your team manage workload and resources effectively?
fc_optionB_label: At times, resource gaps or workload pressure make delivery harder to sustain.
fc_optionB_insightPrompt: Which resource gaps or pressures are having the biggest impact?

questionStem: I can access the information or data I need to make good day-to-day decisions.
insightPrompt: Where is information or data hardest to access?

questionStem: Manual steps in my work are manageable and are handled in the most efficient way possible.
insightPrompt: Which manual steps create the most friction in your work?

questionStem: In the past 30 days, how often did manual tracking, re-entry, or follow-up slow your work down?
insightPrompt: What manual tasks slowed your work down most often?

questionStem: Which statement best reflects how work is usually carried out in your area?
fc_optionA_label: Some helpful data or automation supports the work where it matters most.
fc_optionA_insightPrompt: What is helping data or automation support the work effectively?
fc_optionB_label: Manual coordination, tracking, or workarounds still take up too much effort.
fc_optionB_insightPrompt: What keeps manual coordination, tracking, or workarounds in place?

questionStem: Our digital tools make it reasonably easy to collaborate and keep work moving.
insightPrompt: Where do digital tools fall short in supporting collaboration?

questionStem: The information I need is usually stored in places that make sense and are easy to navigate.
insightPrompt: What makes information hard to locate or navigate?

questionStem: In the past 30 days, how often did you spend extra time looking for information, updates, or the right version of a file?
insightPrompt: What makes it hard to find the right information, update, or file version?

questionStem: Which statement best reflects how your team usually collaborates using digital tools?
fc_optionA_label: Shared tools and channels generally support coordination and visibility.
fc_optionA_insightPrompt: What helps shared tools and channels work well for your team?
fc_optionB_label: Coordination still relies too much on scattered messages or duplicate files.
fc_optionB_insightPrompt: What causes coordination to depend on scattered messages or duplicate files?

questionStem: I feel confident learning and using new digital tools or ways of working when needed.
insightPrompt: What reduces your confidence in learning or using new digital tools?

questionStem: I understand why digital changes are being made and how they are meant to help.
insightPrompt: What feels unclear about why digital changes are happening or how they should help?

questionStem: In the past 30 days, how often did uncertainty or low confidence reduce your willingness to try a digital change?
insightPrompt: What reduced your willingness or confidence to try the change?

questionStem: Which statement best reflects your current readiness for digital change?
fc_optionA_label: I am generally open to digital change when the purpose and support are clear.
fc_optionA_insightPrompt: What helps you most feel ready and willing to try new digital approaches?
fc_optionB_label: I need clearer value, support, or expectations before I feel ready to adopt a digital change.
fc_optionB_insightPrompt: What is creating hesitation or making the value of the change unclear?

questionStem: I know how to use the core systems and tools required for my role.
insightPrompt: Which tools or systems are hardest for you to use confidently?

questionStem: The systems I use work together well enough to avoid unnecessary duplication or rework.
insightPrompt: Where do systems fail to work together well enough?

questionStem: In the past 30 days, how often did system issues, workarounds, or tool confusion slow your work down?
insightPrompt: Which system issues, workarounds, or tool gaps slowed your work down most often?

questionStem: On my team, there is shared understanding of what is changing and how it impacts day-to-day work.
insightPrompt: What makes it hard to create clarity for your team when things change?

questionStem: On my team, people receive support when adapting to changes in their work.
insightPrompt: What limits your ability to coach people through change effectively?

questionStem: In the past 30 days, how often did your team have time or space to practice or learn new ways of working?
insightPrompt: What prevented you from creating more space for practice or learning?

questionStem: Which statement best reflects how change is introduced on your team?
fc_optionA_label: Structured: Change is introduced with clear context and planning before rollout.
fc_optionA_insightPrompt: What helps you provide context, support, and follow-through when change is introduced?
fc_optionB_label: Agile:  Change is introduced quickly, with clarity and structure developed as the team adapts.
fc_optionB_insightPrompt: What support or follow-through is missing when change is introduced on your team?

questionStem: Which statement best reflects how setbacks or challenges during change are percieved?
fc_optionA_label: Challenges are treated as learning opportunities that improve the next attempt.
fc_optionA_insightPrompt: What helps your team learn from challenges productively?
fc_optionB_label: Setbacks and challenges frustrate the team and may discourage perserverance.
fc_optionB_insightPrompt: What reactions to setbacks are discouraging learning or experimentation?

questionStem: On my team, people feel comfortable raising concerns, mistakes, or ideas.
insightPrompt: What makes it hard to create a safe environment for speaking up?

questionStem: On my team, workload and wellbeing signals are noticed.
insightPrompt: What makes it difficult to notice or respond to workload and wellbeing signals early?

questionStem: In the past 30 days, how often were concerns or stress signals followed up on?
insightPrompt: What prevented you from following up more consistently?

questionStem: Which statement best reflects the climate on your team?
fc_optionA_label: People can raise concerns and trust they will be heard and handled appropriately.
fc_optionA_insightPrompt: What helps people trust that concerns will be heard and addressed?
fc_optionB_label: People filter what they say because they worry about negative consequences or inaction.
fc_optionB_insightPrompt: What is making people cautious about speaking openly?

questionStem: Which statement best reflects how workload pressure is managed on your team?
fc_optionA_label: Workload pressure is discussed and adjusted before it occurs.
fc_optionA_insightPrompt: What helps your team address workload pressure early?
fc_optionB_label: Workload pressure is often tolerated and has become the "workplace" norm.
fc_optionB_insightPrompt: Why do you believe workload or wellbeing risks are being tolerated and not being addressed?

questionStem: On my team, communication issues or tension are addressed before they escalate.
insightPrompt: What gets in the way of addressing communication breakdowns or tension early?

questionStem: In the past 30 days, how often did you give a feedback to your team that is clear and useful?
insightPrompt: What makes it difficult to provide clear, respectful, useful feedback consistently?

questionStem: In the past 30 days, how often were communication issues or conflicts addressed?
insightPrompt: What prevented you from coaching or intervening sooner?

questionStem: Which statement best reflects how you handle team communication issues?
fc_optionA_label: Issues are addressed directly when they arise.
fc_optionA_insightPrompt: What helps you address team communication issues effectively?
fc_optionB_label: Issues may be prolonged and addressed after some time.
fc_optionB_insightPrompt: What causes communication issues to be avoided or addressed too late?

questionStem: Which statement best reflects the feedback culture on your team?
fc_optionA_label: Feedback is shared regularly as part of the way we work, collaborate and communicate.
fc_optionA_insightPrompt: What helps create a healthy feedback culture on your team?
fc_optionB_label: Feedback is shared formally in a structured way during performance reviews or when issues arise.
fc_optionB_insightPrompt: What gets in the way of regular, constructive feedback on your team?

questionStem: On my team, priorities are translated into clear actions and expectations.
insightPrompt: What makes it difficult to translate organizational priorities into clear team actions?

questionStem: When priorities compete, our team members have a clear understanding on what comes first.
insightPrompt: What gets in the way of helping your team navigate competing demands?

questionStem: In the past 30 days, how often did your team continue work that no longer aligned to the most important priorities?
Which statement best reflects how priorities are managed on your team?
fc_optionA_label: Priorities are translated clearly enough that people understand what matters most.
fc_optionA_insightPrompt: What helps you create clarity around what matters most?
fc_optionB_label: Changes in priorities create confusion and demand for other tasks often emerge.
fc_optionB_insightPrompt: What is driving competing requests or diluted focus on your team?

questionStem: Which statement best reflects how new work is handled?
fc_optionA_label: New work is weighed against current capacity and commitments.
fc_optionA_insightPrompt: What helps you balance new requests against current commitments?
fc_optionB_label: New work is added quickly even when the team is already stretched.
fc_optionB_insightPrompt: Why is new work being added without enough trade-off or capacity review?

questionStem: On my team, roles, responsibilities, and decision points are clearly understood.
insightPrompt: Where do roles, handoffs, or decision points remain unclear?

questionStem: In the past 30 days, how often did your team have friction or confusion that slows down the team? if Never -> no need for explanation.
insightPrompt: What makes it hard to address process friction or confusion early?

questionStem: In the past 30 days, how often did unclear ownership, approvals, or handoffs delay your team's work?
Which statement best reflects how work flows across your team?
fc_optionA_label: Work progresses through defined ownership, structured steps, and timely handoffs.
fc_optionA_insightPrompt: Where are handoff gaps causing the most delay?
fc_optionB_label: Work progresses through collaboration and flexible role-sharing.
fc_optionB_insightPrompt: Where are handoff gaps causing the most delay?

questionStem: Which statement best reflects your team's processes?
fc_optionA_label: Common processes are clear enough to support consistent execution across teams.
fc_optionA_insightPrompt: What helps consistency in common work processes?
fc_optionB_label: Common work processes are inconsistent and adapted based on situation and team needs.
fc_optionB_insightPrompt: What else makes processes inconsistent?

questionStem: On my team, workload and capacity are planned with awareness of delivery expectations.
insightPrompt: What gets in the way of managing workload, capacity, or staffing proactively?

questionStem: On my team, people generally have the tools, time, and support needed to complete their work.
insightPrompt: Which supports or resources are not sufficient for your team?

questionStem: In the past 30 days, how often did resource or capacity constraints affect your team's ability to deliver on time or at the expected quality?
Which statement best reflects how your team is resourced?
fc_optionA_label: Capacity and resources are managed well enough to support the work.
fc_optionA_insightPrompt: What helps your team stay adequately resourced?
fc_optionB_label: Capacity gaps or overload regularly put delivery quality, timeliness, or wellbeing at risk.
fc_optionB_insightPrompt: Which capacity gaps or overload pressures are creating the biggest risk?

questionStem: Which statement best reflects how team pressure is handled?
fc_optionA_label: Pressure points are noticed and managed by adjusting workload before it over burdens someone.
fc_optionA_insightPrompt: What helps your team act on pressure points early?
fc_optionB_label: Pressure is managed by increasing effort to meet demands.
fc_optionB_insightPrompt: Why are pressure points not being addressed until after they become an issue?

questionStem: On my team, data or reporting is used to inform decisions and monitor performance.
insightPrompt: What makes it difficult to use data or reporting consistently to guide team decisions?

questionStem: Our team have enough opportunities to improve efficiency (e.g., process improvements, automation, AI).
insightPrompt: What is limiting your ability to reduce manual work through process improvement, automation, or AI?

questionStem: Do you think there is a better way to reduce work time while removing manual tracking, follow-up, or duplicate work?
insightPrompt: Which manual activities consumed the most team time?

questionStem: Which statement best reflects how your team works today?
fc_optionA_label: Data and automation support better decisions and reduce avoidable manual work.
fc_optionA_insightPrompt: What is helping data or automation support the team effectively?
fc_optionB_label: The team still relies heavily on manual coordination, tracking, or repetitive effort.
fc_optionB_insightPrompt: What is keeping manual coordination, tracking, or repetitive work in place?

questionStem: Which statement best reflects the data environment available to you?
fc_optionA_label: Data is accessible enough to support most decisions and priorities.
fc_optionA_insightPrompt: What helps make data accessible and influential in decisions?
fc_optionB_label: Data is fragmented, difficult to access, or not trusted enough to guide decisions.
fc_optionB_insightPrompt: What makes data fragmented, inaccessible, or difficult to trust?

questionStem: On my team, shared tools and communication channels support visibility and coordination for work efficiency.
insightPrompt: What makes it difficult to create visibility and coordination through shared digital practices?

questionStem: In the past 30 days, how often did your team lose time because information, updates, or files were hard to locate or spread across too many places?
Which statement best reflects how your team collaborates digitally?
fc_optionA_label: Work is coordinated through shared tools and structured channels.
fc_optionA_insightPrompt: What helps shared digital practices support teamwork and visibility?
fc_optionB_label: Work is coordinated through various channels and is inconsistent across the organization.
fc_optionB_insightPrompt: What is driving inconistency in work coordination?

questionStem: On my team, there is clarity on why digital changes are happening and what success looks like.
insightPrompt: What makes it difficult to create understanding of why digital changes are happening?

questionStem: In the past 30 days, how often did team mention about positive adoption of a digital change (advantages such as efficiency improvement, time managetment)?
insightPrompt: What most often caused hesitation or low confidence?

questionStem: Which statement best reflects your team's readiness for digital change?
fc_optionA_label: The team is generally willing to adopt new digital approaches when the value and support are clear.
fc_optionA_insightPrompt: What helps your team feel ready to adopt digital changes?
fc_optionB_label: The team is hesitant because the value, support, or expectations are not clear enough.
fc_optionB_insightPrompt: What do you believe is making the value, support, or expectations unclear for your team?

questionStem: On my team, people have the capability and ongoing support to use core systems and tools.
insightPrompt: What is limiting your team's capability or support to use core systems and tools effectively?

questionStem: In the past 30 days, how often did tool confusion, system limitations, or workarounds reduce your team's efficiency?
Which statement best reflects the tools and systems your team relies on?
fc_optionA_label: Tools and systems are integrated enough to support most workflows.
fc_optionA_insightPrompt: What is helping tools and systems support your team effectively?
fc_optionB_label: Tools and systems often create friction, duplication, or avoidable workarounds.
fc_optionB_insightPrompt: What is creating the most friction, duplication, or workarounds for your team?

questionStem: Leaders create enough strategic clarity that people understand why change is happening and what it requires.
insightPrompt: What is limiting strategic clarity around change?

questionStem: Leaders adapt direction, invesment, and support effectively when conditions change
insightPrompt: What makes it difficult to adjust direction, investment, or support when conditions change?

questionStem: Leadership approaches encourage people feel confident navigating change, not just compliance
insightPrompt: What is getting in the way of building confidence, not just compliance, during change?

questionStem: In the past 30 days, how often did leaders remove a barrier or make a decision that helped teams adapt more quickly?
insightPrompt: What prevented leaders from removing barriers or making decisions sooner?

questionStem: Leaders model behaviours that make it safe to raise risks, concerns, and mistakes.
insightPrompt: What leadership behaviours may be discouraging people from raising risks, concerns, or mistakes?

questionStem: Do leaders treat psychological safety, respect, and well-being as essential leadership responsibilities?
insightPrompt: Where is psychological safety, respect, or wellbeing being treated as optional?

questionStem: Do leaders respond to pressure or conduct risks before they harm trust or performance?
insightPrompt: What gets in the way of responding to pressure, workload, or conduct risks earlier?

questionStem: In the past 30 days, how often did leaders act on a signal related to burnout risk, poor behaviour, or team safety?
insightPrompt: What prevented earlier action on burnout risk, poor behaviour, or team safety signals?

questionStem: Which statement best reflects the leadership climate?
fc_optionA_label: People can raise difficult issues and expect leaders to listen and respond constructively.
fc_optionA_insightPrompt: What is helping leaders respond constructively to difficult issues?
fc_optionB_label: I believe people filter what they say because they expect negative consequences.
fc_optionB_insightPrompt: What do you believe is causing people to filter what they say to leaders?

questionStem: Communicating honestly and consistently is always valued during uncertainty
insightPrompt: What gets in the way of honest, consistent communication during uncertainty?

questionStem: How often do leaders address misalignment, conflict, or unproductive dynamics that affect performance?
insightPrompt: What makes it difficult for leaders to address misalignment, conflict, or unproductive dynamics?

questionStem: Leadership interactions reinforce accountability, respect, and constructive challenge.
insightPrompt: Where are leadership interactions falling short of accountability, respect, or constructive challenge?

questionStem: In the past 30 days, how often did leaders address a cross-team tension, leadership misalignment, or trust issues?
insightPrompt: What prevented leaders from addressing tension, misalignment, or trust issues sooner?

questionStem: Which statement best reflects how leadership communication is experienced across the organization?
fc_optionA_label: Leadership communication is clear, candid, and aligned enough to build trust across the organization
fc_optionA_insightPrompt: What is helping leadership communication build trust?
fc_optionB_label: Leadership communication is mixed, overly guarded, or inconsistent across leaders.
fc_optionB_insightPrompt: What is causing leadership communication to feel mixed, guarded, or inconsistent?

questionStem: Strategic priorities are clear enough to guide decisions, trade-offs, and investment choices.
insightPrompt: What is making strategic priorities unclear or difficult to use in decisions and trade-offs?
fc_optionA_label: Leaders are aligned enough on what matter most that their messages and decisions are consistent

questionStem: Do leaders make effective trade-offs when new priorities emerge instead of adding more work?
insightPrompt: What gets in the way of making trade-offs when new priorities emerge?
fc_optionA_label: Structures and processes largely support efficient executition rather than  create unnecessary friction, duplication, or delay.

questionStem: Priorities remain aligned across leaders strongly enough to avoid confusion lower in the organization.
insightPrompt: Where is leadership alignment around priorities breaking down?

questionStem: In the past 30 days, how often did leaders stop or deprioritize work to protect top priorities?
insightPrompt: What prevented leaders from stopping or deferring lower-value work more often?

questionStem: Which statement best reflects how aligned leaders are on what matters most?
fc_optionA_label: Leaders are aligned enough that messages and decisions are consistent.
fc_optionA_insightPrompt: What is helping leaders stay aligned on what matters most?
fc_optionB_label: Leaders send mixed signals or protect different priorities sometimes creating confusion.
fc_optionB_insightPrompt: What is causing mixed signals across leadership?

questionStem: Is accountability, governance and decision rights clear enough for work to move efficiently across organization?
insightPrompt: Where are accountability, governance, or decision rights unclear?

questionStem: Does cross-functional work happen smoothly without avoidable delays or rework?
insightPrompt: Where is cross-functional coordination breaking down?

questionStem: Do leaders take action to remove bottlenecks or process barriers that slow execution?
insightPrompt: What makes it difficult to address structural friction, bottlenecks, or duplication?

questionStem: In the past 30 days, how often did leaders intervene to resolve a bottleneck, role ambiguity, or cross-functional blockage affecting delivery?
insightPrompt: What prevented leaders from intervening sooner to resolve the blockage?

questionStem: Which statement best reflects the organization's operating design?
fc_optionA_label: Structures and processes largely support efficient execution.
fc_optionA_insightPrompt: What is helping structures and processes support efficient execution?
fc_optionB_label: Structures or processes create unnecessary friction, duplication, or delay.
fc_optionB_insightPrompt: What parts of the structure or process design are creating the most friction?

questionStem: Resources are allocated in ways that reflect strategic priorities and real capacity constraints.
insightPrompt: Where is resource allocation drifting away from priorities or real capacity?

questionStem: The organization has enough capability, capacity, and support in critical areas to deliver on its commitments.
insightPrompt: Which capability, capacity, or support gaps are most threatening delivery?

questionStem: In the past 30 days, how often did leaders reallocate resources, funding, or decision support to address a delivery risk?
insightPrompt: What prevented faster or more decisive resource reallocation?

questionStem: Are leadership decisions guided by reliable data instead of intuition or escalation?
insightPrompt: What is limiting the use of reliable data in leadership decisions?

questionStem: Leaders invest in automation, AI, or process improvement where it can meaningfully reduce friction or increase insight.
insightPrompt: What gets in the way of investing in automation, AI, or process improvement where it would help most?

questionStem: In the past 30 days, how often did manual reporting or coordination slow important decisions?
insightPrompt: Which manual reporting, coordination, or automation gaps had the biggest impact?

questionStem: The organization's data environment is reliable and accesible enough to support decision-making
insightPrompt: What is keeping data and automation from being embedded in real workflows and decisions?

questionStem: Do digital channels and collaboration tools support effective teamwork?
insightPrompt: Where are digital channels, collaboration tools, or information practices failing to support coordination?

questionStem: Important information is organized and governed well enough to serve as a reliable source of truth.
insightPrompt: Where is information governance or source-of-truth discipline breaking down?

questionStem: In the past 30 days, how often did fragmented communication, scattered files, or poor visibility slow coordination across teams?
insightPrompt: What most often caused fragmented communication or poor visibility across teams?

questionStem: Leaders create enough understanding of digital direction that people know why changes matter.
insightPrompt: What is limiting understanding of the organization's digital direction?

questionStem: Does the organization have enough confidence and support to adopt digital change?
insightPrompt: What is reducing confidence or slowing willingness to adopt digital change?

questionStem: In the past 30 days, how often did hesitation, uncertainty, or competing priorities slow a digital change effort?
insightPrompt: What most often caused hesitation, uncertainty, or slow movement on digital change?

questionStem: Core platforms and systems support productivity well enough across the organization.
insightPrompt: Where are core platforms or systems falling short of supporting productivity?

questionStem: The organization's systems are integrated well enough to avoid excessive duplication, re-entry, or workarounds.
insightPrompt: Where are systems not integrated well enough?
